questions.question: Receiving inspection performed by a buyer's representative on parts arriving from a supplier is an example of:
questions.explanation: Customer quality control is the buyer protecting its interests through source or receiving inspection, confirming the supplier met purchase-order requirements.

questions.question: Before applying liquid penetrant or magnetic-particle testing, why is VT-verified surface finish important?
options: It has no effect on other methods
A rougher surface always improves their sensitivity
Finish only matters for cosmetic appearance
Those methods require a properly prepared, clean surface to work
questions.explanation: Penetrant and magnetic-particle testing both demand a properly prepared surface; poor finish can mask discontinuities and produce false or missed indications.

questions.question: Wall thinning measured on an operating pressure vessel is judged acceptable until it reaches a calculated minimum. This reflects:
questions.explanation: In-service VT is governed by in-service and fitness-for-service rules; thinning that would never have shipped new can be acceptable on operating equipment until it crosses a calculated minimum.

questions.question: Which is the best definition of a discontinuity?
options: Any flaw that is automatically rejectable
Any interruption in the normal structure of a material, which may or may not be a defect
A deviation that only occurs in service
questions.explanation: A discontinuity is any interruption in the normal structure; it is classed a defect only when evaluation against acceptance criteria finds it rejectable.

questions.question: The natural blind spot in each eye is caused by:
options: The point where the optic nerve exits the retina, lacking photoreceptors
questions.explanation: The optic nerve exits through the retina at a spot with no photoreceptors, producing a blind spot; deliberate scanning compensates for it.

questions.question: Approximately how long does full dark adaptation of the eye take?
questions.explanation: Dark adaptation is slow - roughly thirty minutes for the rods to reach full sensitivity - so dim examinations require time and supplemental light.

questions.question: Widely used direct-VT geometry requires the eye within about 24 inches of the surface and a viewing angle of at least:
questions.explanation: Common direct-VT practice (e.g., ASME Section V, Article 9) places the eye within about 600 millimetres and the line of sight at no less than 30 degrees to the surface.

questions.question: A procedure specifies 1000 lux minimum at the surface for direct VT. How is this confirmed, and what is 1000 lux in footcandles?
options: Assumed from the work lights; about 1000 fc
Measured with a calibrated light meter at the surface; about 100 fc
Measured at the meter's factory; about 1 fc
questions.explanation: Illumination is verified at the surface with a calibrated meter; since 1 footcandle is about 10.76 lux, 1000 lux is roughly 100 footcandles.

questions.question: Shallow surface cracking that is invisible under flat, head-on light is best revealed by:
options: Maximum flat light from directly behind the inspector
Low-angle (oblique) raking light combined with a varied viewing angle
questions.explanation: Oblique raking light casts shadows that bring out surface relief, and changing the viewing angle alters contrast, exposing shallow features flat light washes out.

questions.question: An inspector with unrecognized red-green color deficiency is most likely to struggle with which task?
options: Distinguishing heat-tint colors or a dye-penetrant indication from its developer
Measuring a fillet weld leg with a gauge
Holding the eye within 24 inches of the surface
questions.explanation: Color-vision deficiency impairs distinguishing colors and shades - temper/heat-tint colors, penetrant indications, corrosion products - which is why color perception is screened in qualification.
